title: DOE Announces $9.63 Billion Loan to BlueOval SK to Further Expand US Manufacturing of Electric Vehicle Batteries
content: The U.S. Department of Energyâ€™s Loan Programs Office (LPO) has closed a $9.63 billion loan to BlueOval SK LLC (BOSK), a joint venture between Ford Motor Company and SK On, to build three battery manufacturing plants (one in Tennessee and two in Kentucky). The facilities will enable 120 GWh of annual U.S. battery production, creating 7,500 operations jobs and previously supporting 5,000 construction jobs. This is the largest loan under the Advanced Technology Vehicles Manufacturing (ATVM) Program, advancing domestic EV battery production, reducing reliance on foreign supply chains, and supporting Bidenâ€™s goal of 50% zero-emission vehicles by 2030.
author: Analysts Account
